title: Pluchea dodonaeifolia
taxonomy: Pluchea dodonaeifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Inuleae, a group of plants commonly known as the ironweed or dogfennel tribe. This plant is native to Australia and New Guinea. Pluchea dodonaeifolia is a shrubby herb with small white flowers. The species is often found growing in a variety of habitats, including coastal areas and grasslands.
uses: Pluchea dodonaeifolia has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices. Its leaves and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Its medicinal uses are mainly anecdotal and not extensively documented in Western medical literature.

intro: Pluchea dodonaeifolia, also known as Dodonaea leaf pluchea, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pluchea dodonaeifolia include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions like fever, cough, and skin infections.
